page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-2xCPU-8GB is a general-purpose virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor. The configuration includes 2 shared vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz, with 2 physical cores and 1 thread per core. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes a 16 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates modest performance characteristics. The shared CPU allocation results in poor single-core and multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached read/write operations show weak performance, landing in the bottom 25%. However, single-threaded operations such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression achieve average performance in the middle 50%. Geekbench testing reveals an average single-core score but a weak multi-core score, with software compilation and text processing also ranking in the bottom 25%.

Given the shared CPU resources and lack of local storage, this server is positioned as a budget-friendly option for workloads with low resource intensity. It is suitable for lightweight tasks such as basic web serving, development environments, simple text processing, or small-scale Redis caching. It is not recommended for high-throughput database operations, intensive multi-threaded compilation, or large-scale LLM inference, where its performance falls into the bottom 10% to 25% brackets.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-2xCPU-8GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7542 processor. It features 2 shared vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 8.0 GB of system memory, and a 16 MB L3 cache. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs. Performance benchmarks show weak to poor results for multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-threaded compression tasks perform at an average level. This resource profile is qualitatively cost-effective for low-intensity workloads. It is best suited for lightweight applications, development environments, basic text processing, and small-scale caching rather than compute-heavy or storage-intensive production databases.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server with shared AMD EPYC processors and balanced memory for lightweight development and basic web hosting.
